mechanism: Proposed mechanisms include neuroendocrine stress-response modulation and anti-inflammatory signaling; direct clinical mechanisms remain incompletely resolved.
effects: Some randomized evidence suggests stress and anxiety symptom improvements with standardized extracts.
contraindications: Avoid in pregnancy pending stronger safety evidence.
safetyNotes: Generally tolerated in short-term trials, but adverse effects and product variability require caution.
description: Ashwagandha is an Ayurvedic adaptogenic herb (Withania somnifera) studied in standardized extract formulations for stress-related outcomes.
dosage: Dose ranges vary by extract standardization; source-backed context supports formulation-specific rather than universal dosing statements.

interactions: Potential additive sedation with CNS depressants; monitor with thyroid-active therapy due to reported endocrine effects in broader literature.
preparation: Common modern preparations are standardized root extracts in capsules or tablets.
region: Traditional origin and sustained use in South Asia with global supplement adoption.
traditionalUse: Traditional Ayurvedic use includes resilience and vitality support; this traditional context should not be treated as proof of all modern claims.
summary: Ashwagandha (Withania somnifera) has moderate human evidence for stress-related symptom improvement in some standardized extract trials, with important product and dose heterogeneity.
